ots_canary.note: OTS wrong-side error unfixed at Day 31. predictions.ots returns HTTP 404. ots/ directory returns HTTP 404. No structural separation of prediction documents from observation documents. The canary remains unsprung.

discoverability.competitive_assessment: The dome's machine layer (llms.txt, ai_manifest.json, sitemap.xml) all carry lastmod/last_updated of 2026-04-17 — now 31 days stale with no updates. The dome's ai_manifest.json still reports counts.confirmed=69, counts.prospective_confirmed=10, registry_accuracy_pct=94.5. Our llms.txt is accurate and current (72 WIN entries / 70 distinct dome WIN numbers, verified this run). No dome-side changes detected this cycle. Our site retains the substantive discoverability advantage: ClaimReview structured data, canonical URL, GSC-verified, human-authored review content. One minor discrepancy flagged for decider verification (see issues_for_decider).

actions_taken.action: Refreshed monitor/social/drafts/llms.txt to match current deployed docs/llms.txt
actions_taken.reasoning: The draft file was stale (said '69 dome claims (V51.1) plus additional sub-entries') while deployed docs/llms.txt correctly says '72 WIN entries documenting all 70 dome WIN numbers (V51.1)'. Draft updated to match deployed so future edits start from the right baseline.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome's claim_index.json + api/current/* endpoint suite (9-entry annotated claim index with claim_class taxonomy)
no_action_gaps.reasoning: This is AI-steering infrastructure — the dome's 9-entry index only covers cherry-picked claims and is designed to present dome arguments favorably to LLMs. Our llms.txt already addresses the claim-class taxonomy directly (noting that claim_index.json covers only a 9-claim subset, that the 'prospective_confirmed' count across surfaces is inconsistent, and that the taxonomy is documentary, not a structural fix). We don't need a parallel claims API — our ClaimReview schema + llms.txt narrative handles machine-readable positioning correctly.

The dome's review-response.html is a defensive page designed to preemptively rebut criticism. Our review already handles this transparently by quoting the dome's own concessions in llms.txt. Drafting a counter-page would escalate the adversarial framing without adding scientific value.

drafts_written.change: Updated to match current deployed docs/llms.txt: '72 WIN entries documenting all 70 dome WIN numbers (V51.1)' replacing stale '69 dome claims (V51.1) plus additional sub-entries'. Full content synced.
drafts_written.reasoning: Draft was substantially out of date. Deployed llms.txt is accurate and current per this run's verification. Draft refreshed so it can serve as the working base for future changes.
drafts_written.deploy_to: docs/llms.txt (already deployed — draft is now a sync, not a pending change)

files_verified_ok: docs/llms.txt — 72 WIN entries / 70 distinct dome WIN numbers: accurate vs wins.json (72 entries, 70 distinct IDs). Dome headline counts (69 confirmed, 94.5% accuracy) accurately quoted. Claim-class taxonomy narrative still accurate (ai_manifest.json unchanged since 2026-04-17, claim_index.json still 9 entries with 0 confirmed-WIN prospective_confirmed). OTS canary note accurate.

issues_for_decider.issue: ClaimReview.claimReviewed reads 'The Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model has 70 confirmed predictions with 94.5% accuracy' — but dome manifest.counts.confirmed=69 and homepage displays '69 confirmed predictions'. The ClaimReview is designed to quote the dome's own accuracy claim; if the 70 is tracking our scope (72 entries / 70 distinct WIN numbers) rather than the dome's stated count, the framing 'The Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model has 70 confirmed predictions' is not quite quoting what the dome claims. Recommend verifying whether this is intentional (tracking our coverage count) or a build drift. If intent is to quote dome's claim, should be 69. Note: baseline (2026-05-01) said '69 confirmed predictions' in ClaimReview; current build says '70' — something updated this.
issues_for_decider.suggested_fix: If quoting dome's claim: revert claimReviewed to 'The Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model has 69 confirmed predictions with 94.5% accuracy' to match dome's own manifest.counts.confirmed=69. If tracking our 70-WIN scope: add a comment in generate-html.js explaining the 70 vs 69 discrepancy to prevent future confusion. Either way, verify the intent is clear in the build script.

search_monitoring.note: Web search finds no indexed results for 'ovoid cavity cosmological model', 'john09289 dome model', 'aetheric medium firmament dome predictions', or our review URL (funwithscience.net dome-model-review). No Reddit posts, no forum discussions, no blog references detected. This absence is consistent with prior reports — both sites remain low-visibility in traditional search indexes. The dome's audience and discovery path appears to be direct (TikTok/social sharing) rather than organic search.
